methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Theion is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Ulrich Ehmes that has raised €25M+ to date. The comparable universe consists of one public company (LNZA) and eight private peers operating in the same sector and stage. The primary valuation methodology applied is peer-median analysis across three directly comparable Series A firms in Climate & Energy: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The peer-median method yields a central valuation of $150M, derived from the median of the three comparable valuations. The range across these comps spans from $110M to $200M, establishing boundaries for the analysis. This single-method approach implies a $150M fair value, with modest downside adjustment to $128M and upside adjustment to $172M reflecting the range observed in the peer set.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, with a median point of $150M. Key uncertainties include the limited depth of the comparable set (three firms), the heterogeneity typically observed within early-stage climate-tech subsectors, and the materiality of Theion's specific technology, capital efficiency, and path to commercialization relative to the selected peers. Additional comparable data or revenue/cash-burn metrics would refine confidence in this range.
